retry_reason: Put operations are release operations; if the refcount was already zero, calling put is a use-after-free bug – no retry is possible or meaningful.
contract_summary: Releases one reference to a bpf_trampoline. If the reference count drops to zero, the trampoline is fully cleaned up: it is removed from global hash lists, its direct_ops are freed, and the memory is freed. The caller must guarantee that all attached programs have been detached before the final put. After bpf_trampoline_put returns, the caller must not access the trampoline pointer. The function acquires the global trampoline_mutex internally; no locks need to be held by the caller. This is a standard reference-count release (put) operation: each successful get or alloc must be paired with exactly one put.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to decrement the reference count of a bpf_trampoline and free it when the count reaches zero. All other actions (removing from hash lists, freeing ops, kfree) are direct consequences of the final reference being released.

check.reason: The refcount_dec_and_test call on &tr->refcnt is a put operation on parameter tr. The access path tr.refcnt.refs and type chain bpf_trampoline--refcount_t--atomic_t match the code and callee information.
